trg-international.title: First internship
trg-international.subtitle: TRG International
trg-international.description: I joined the HR team to learn soft skills, but I utilized my coding skills to create apps that improve my team's workflow. I represented TRG at career fairs, participated in CSR activities, and helped organise events for the company, even one involving the CEO himself!

utm-career-center.title: On-campus job
utm-career-center.subtitle: UTM Career Center
utm-career-center.description: As tech support, I ensured that all materials were accessible to all 16,000+ students. I also helped with industry outreach, organized career fairs, guided students in resume building and job searching.

go-on-hacks.title: Software hackathon win 🏆
go-on-hacks.subtitle: Go On Hacks
go-on-hacks.description: I built my first ever Google extension for this hackathon, since it was a "stupid hackathon", we decided to create a tool that turns every face on your screen into LeBron James. I learnt so many new skills and met very ambitious people, from founders to hackers!

ember-hacks.title: Organizing a hackathon
ember-hacks.subtitle: EmberHacks
ember-hacks.description: I organized my first hackathon for 100+ amazing hackers. I stayed back overnight to guide them, hosted minigames, and had a blast!

university-of-toronto.title: University of Toronto
university-of-toronto.subtitle: my realization about university
university-of-toronto.description: i study comp sci at the uoft mississauga, but i usually call myself uoft (shhh no one knows), i am trying my very best to hang on to a 3.58 gpa, i realized that the most important thing from university is not the knowledge but the friends and networking you grow along the way!

techto-hackathon.title: Quickest hackathon ever
techto-hackathon.subtitle: TechTO Hackathon
techto-hackathon.description: We had 6 hours to create a functioning app. Somehow, I ended up creating a tool that is extremely helpful for healthcare, with the help of my amazing teammates.

hack-the-north.title: Canada's biggest hackathon
hack-the-north.subtitle: Hack The North
hack-the-north.description: I stayed for 2 nights at this hackathon, competed with over 1200+ hackers, and witnessed a World record being broken! It was super tiring, I felt sick afterwards, but it was worth it.

ignition-hacks.title: Hosting a hackathon workshop
ignition-hacks.subtitle: Ignition Hacks
ignition-hacks.description: I hosted my first ever hackathon workshop for 100+ hackers. I taught them how to use Mapbox, the tool which I am using for the map on this website!

aws-summit.title: AWS's largest event
aws-summit.subtitle: AWS Summit
aws-summit.description: I arrived here at 7am and stayed until 5pm, with over 10,000 attendees. I learned so much information from industry experts. I got a chance to train my own AI model, with a chance to win a trip to Vegas (I didn't win).

google-devfest.title: Huge Google workshop
google-devfest.subtitle: Google DevFest
google-devfest.description: I participated in a Gemini workshop with 500+ guests, stood up from the crowd to ask questions. I learnt so much about the power of AI and how to use it in my projects!

murder-mystery-party.title: Party for founders
murder-mystery-party.subtitle: Murder Mystery Party
murder-mystery-party.description: I was 4,500km from home, but I attended a Halloween party for young entrepreneurs. I wore a mask the entire night (completely anonymous), and I met so many interesting and ambitious people!

shut-up-and-code.title: Meeting strangers to code
shut-up-and-code.subtitle: Shut Up & Code
shut-up-and-code.description: Strangers just met up to code and learn from each other. I was a bit shy at first, but I ended up meeting some amazing people there.

wallyhacks.title: First hackathon win 🏆
wallyhacks.subtitle: Wally Hacks
wallyhacks.description: This was my first ever hackathon, and I won second place. I learnt so much about the hackathon process, and I met so many amazing people.

codequest.title: CodeQuest
codequest.subtitle: leetcode competition
codequest.description: i was speedrunning through the questions and got first place!

bell-sap-case-competition.subtitle: first ever case competition
bell-sap-case-competition.description: due to traffic, i booked a $50 uber from toronto to mississauga, arrived 40 minutes late, but i still attend, and the company representatives notice my energy, they invited me to a private coffee chat after the event!

deltahacks.subtitle: 1st hackathon in the streak
deltahacks.description:
- didn't win but this is one of my favourite projects ever
- we analyzed flight paths of 3m+ different flights to see how much fuels we can save if planes fly in birds formation
- so cool to see how nature works

amazon-fulfillment-center.subtitle: 2 hour trip opened my eyes
amazon-fulfillment-center.description:
- it was super cool to see the most advanced robotics being used here
- we weren't albe
